articles.title: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
articles.summary: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

articles.title: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
articles.summary: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

articles.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
articles.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

articles.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
articles.summary: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".

articles.title: Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body
articles.summary: Edgar Lungu's family and the Zambian government remained in dispute 12 months after he died.

articles.title: Spider which uses spring trap to capture prey discovered in Australia
articles.summary: Researchers say their hunting method - which allows them to prey on dangerous ants - is unprecedented.

articles.title: US top court says Rastafarian man cannot sue prison guards who cut his dreadlocks
articles.summary: The former Louisiana inmate argued his Rastafarian faith was violated after prison officials focibly shaved off his hair.

articles.title: Irish government to contribute £197m to cross-border rail services
articles.summary: The money will come from the last allocation from the Irish government's Shared Island Fund.

articles.title: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
articles.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

articles.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
articles.summary: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.
